heading: An elevated floor with room to work underneath
paragraphs: A raised access floor is an elevated floor area built above another floor — typically a concrete slab. It comprises load-bearing floor panels laid in a horizontal grid, supported by adjustable vertical pedestals.
This creates an underfloor void to cost-effectively house and distribute your building services. Panels are readily removable, so access to everything beneath is fast and easy.
diagramCaption: Cross-section: load-bearing panels on adjustable pedestals over the structural slab.
